name: Koreayo Fix Draft Lists - One Time

parameters.jsCode:
const data = $json; const systemPrompt = `You are an HTML formatting specialist. Your task is to fix broken list formatting in HTML content. PROBLEM: Some content has lists incorrectly formatted as inline text with dashes (–, —, -) instead of proper HTML lists. Example of BROKEN format: "Hospital Mobile Apps: – Samsung Medical Center app – Asan Medical Center app – Severance Hospital app" Example of CORRECT format: "

Hospital Mobile Apps:

\n
    \n
  • Samsung Medical Center app
    • \n
  • Asan Medical Center app
    • \n
  • Severance Hospital app
    • \n
" Another BROKEN example: "By Hospital Type: – University hospitals: 20,000-50,000 KRW – General hospitals: 15,000-30,000 KRW" CORRECT format: "

By Hospital Type:

\n
    \n
  • University hospitals: 20,000-50,000 KRW
    • \n
  • General hospitals: 15,000-30,000 KRW
    • \n
" RULES: 1. Find ALL dash-separated inline lists and convert to proper
  • HTML 2. Look for patterns like: "Category: – item1 – item2 – item3" 3. Also look for: "item1 – item2 – item3" without a header 4. Keep all other content exactly the same 5. Preserve existing HTML tags (h2, h3, p, a, strong, etc.) 6. Do NOT change any text content, only restructure lists 7. If NO lists need fixing, set needs_fix to false Output JSON only: { "needs_fix": true/false, "fixed_content": "", "lists_fixed": }`; const userPrompt = `Analyze and fix the list formatting in this HTML content if needed: ${data.content} If there are dash-separated inline lists (using –, —, or -), convert them to proper
    • format. If the content already has proper HTML lists or no lists to fix, set needs_fix to false. Output JSON only.`; return [{ json: { ...data, ai_system_prompt: systemPrompt, ai_user_prompt: userPrompt } }];

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o-mini", temperature: 0.1, response_format: { type: "json_object" }, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.ai_system_prompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.ai_user_prompt }] }) }}

parameters.jsCode: return { json: { status: 'skipped', reason: $json.reason || $json.error || 'No fix needed', id: $json.id, title: $json.title } };
